channel.title: Cybersecurity - UniBO ISI (study podcast)
channel.itunes:author: UniBO ISI study notes
channel.description: A two-host study podcast covering the Cybersecurity course (Module 1 + AI module + mandatory readings) for the ISI master at the University of Bologna. AI-narrated study aid, not an official source.
channel.itunes:summary: A two-host study podcast covering the Cybersecurity course (Module 1 + AI module + mandatory readings) for the ISI master at the University of Bologna. AI-narrated study aid, not an official source.

item.title: 1. Introduction to Computer Security
item.description: What computer security is, the CIA triad, security terminology, the design principles, attack surfaces and attack trees.
item.itunes:summary: What computer security is, the CIA triad, security terminology, the design principles, attack surfaces and attack trees.

item.title: 2. Security Concepts and Terminology
item.description: Threats versus attacks, the four threat consequences, assets, the thirteen design principles, and security strategy.
item.itunes:summary: Threats versus attacks, the four threat consequences, assets, the thirteen design principles, and security strategy.

item.title: 3. Cryptographic Tools
item.description: Symmetric and public-key cryptography, DES and AES, hash functions and MACs, digital signatures, certificates and PKI.
item.itunes:summary: Symmetric and public-key cryptography, DES and AES, hash functions and MACs, digital signatures, certificates and PKI.

item.title: 4. User Authentication
item.description: The four means of authentication, passwords, salting and hashing, tokens and smart cards, biometrics, MFA and passkeys.
item.itunes:summary: The four means of authentication, passwords, salting and hashing, tokens and smart cards, biometrics, MFA and passkeys.

item.title: 5. Cybersecurity of AI
item.description: Adversarial examples, FGSM and PGD, transferability, defenses, LLM privacy, prompt injection and agentic-AI risks.
item.itunes:summary: Adversarial examples, FGSM and PGD, transferability, defenses, LLM privacy, prompt injection and agentic-AI risks.

item.title: 6. Mandatory Readings (Exam Question 5)
item.description: The five exam readings: Thompson's Trusting Trust, the Morris worm, Schneier on IoT, the EU Cyber Resilience Act, and e-voting risks.
item.itunes:summary: The five exam readings: Thompson's Trusting Trust, the Morris worm, Schneier on IoT, the EU Cyber Resilience Act, and e-voting risks.
